mechanism_narrative: Low and stable federal funds rates indicate an accommodative monetary environment, reducing the cost of capital for businesses and encouraging investment, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative on average when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and potential corporate margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as investors anticipate rate hikes.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% is near full employment
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive on average when UNRATE is below 4.8% over any 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with robust housing starts indicates broad-based economic demand and consumer confidence, which fuels corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2% YoY AND HOUST > 1000k units
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative on average when both GDPC1 growth is > 2% YoY and HOUST > 1000k units over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation regime, a slight uptick in CPI suggests healthy demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as investors anticipate continued growth.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative on average when CPIAUCSL monthly change is between 0.1% and 0.3% in a low inflation regime (e.g., FEDFUNDS < 2%), this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high retail sales indicate robust consumer spending, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average when RSAFS is above its 12-month moving average over any 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in non-farm payrolls signals strong job creation and economic health, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as market sentiment improves.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative on average when PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 200k over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust industrial production coupled with strong retail sales indicates a healthy balance between supply and demand, suggesting sustainable economic growth and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth > 2% AND RSAFS YoY growth > 3%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative on average when both INDPRO YoY growth > 2% and RSAFS YoY growth > 3% over any 4-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, accelerating GDP growth signals a strong rebound in economic activity, which translates into rapidly improving corporate fundamentals and leads to significant positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average when GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth exceeds 3% during a confirmed post-recession period, this hypothesis is rejected.
